sectionHeadings: 009. Tenant self-hosted cron: the in-app scheduler module
FR-003, the lock and the scale tiers
FR-005, productionized daemon start (no hardcoded loopback)
record.summary: The adapter-side companion to OAP spec 230. Encore's CronJob primitive is a no-op on self-hosted deploys (there is no Encore Cloud scheduler), so every tenant CronJob is silent. This module installs an in-app scheduler service into the generated Encore app: a task_schedules store in the base app's single SQLDatabase("app") (INV-11, no per-service db), register/list endpoints, and a polling daemon that fires due jobs against the app's own endpoints. The daemon fires each due job exactly once across replicas via a lock tiered on the tenant's create-project scale choice: a Postgres atomic claim by default (small scale, zero extra infra), a Redis distributed lock when the typed REDIS_HOST connection is configured (large scale). One code path, tier auto-detected by the baseline lib/redis client (REDIS_HOST/REDIS_USER/ REDIS_PASSWORD; there is no REDIS_URL). The module therefore requires data-postgres (always) and optionally peers with data-redis (large tier only). Distinct from stagecraft's own control-plane sweepers (OAP spec 224), which stay on per-sweeper K8s CronJobs.
record.title: Tenant self-hosted cron: the in-app scheduler module (postgres small-scale / +redis large-scale)
